# tokens: # Barrier dispatch. Each Signature declares BarrierPos B — the count
# of leading args (sig[0..B-1]) that MAY be filled from forward tokens,
# in source order; the rest (sig[B..N-1]) always come from the stack,
# top-down. So B=0 is legacy stack-only, B=N is legacy forward-prec,
# and 0 < B < N mixes the two. Call-site modifiers override B:
#   /f  → B becomes N (all forward) AND is strict: if the forward scan
#         can't fill every slot the sig is rejected — no stack fallback.
#   /K  → only consider overloads whose total arg count is K.
#   /Kf, /Ks → the /K arity filter combined with /f or /s.
# The handler always sees args in signature order: args[0] is whatever
# bound to sig[0], args[1] to sig[1], … regardless of which side it
# came from. (Plain /s and /f on a 2-arg forward-prec word are also
# covered in force.tsv; this file widens to arities 0..7, off-centre

note: a 0-arg word fires immediately, consuming nothing
0-arg word leaves the prior stack value untouched
0-arg word consumes no forward token; 1 runs after
/0 selects the 0-arg overload (matched via the fallback path)
